raw_outputs.groq:
```json
{
  "preconditions": [
    "x and y must be lists of numbers",
    "x and y must have the same length",
    "x and y must not be empty"
  ],
  "postconditions": [
    "the result is the covariance between x and y",
    "the result is a floating point number"
  ],
  "edge_cases": [
    "x and y have only one element",
    "x and y have identical elements",
    "x and y have elements with extreme values"
  ]
}
```
raw_outputs.groq2:
```json
{
  "preconditions": [
    "x and y must be lists of strings",
    "x and y must have the same length",
    "x and y must not be empty"
  ],
  "postconditions": [
    "returns a float representing the covariance between x and y",
    "the result is a valid covariance value"
  ],
  "edge_cases": [
    "if x or y is empty, returns NaN",
    "if x and y have different lengths, raises a ValueError"
  ]
}
```
raw_outputs.hf:
```json
{
  "preconditions": [
    "x and y must be lists of strings",
    "x and y must have the same length",
    "x and y must not be empty"
  ],
  "postconditions": [
    "returns a float representing the covariance between x and y",
    "the result is a valid covariance value"
  ],
  "edge_cases": [
    "if x or y is empty, raises a StatisticsError",
    "if x and y have different lengths, raises a StatisticsError"
  ]
}
```
